lines: Place your feet flat on the floor if you're sitting, or press your palms together if you're lying down. [short pause] Feel the contact. [short pause] The pressure. [short pause] Something solid.

Now breathe with me. [short pause] In through your nose for five... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] And out through your mouth for seven... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven.

Again. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] And out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven.

One more. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven.
Good. [short pause] You're here. [short pause] Present. [short pause] Grounded.
Now listen to this. [short pause] God speaking to Joshua, who was about to lead an entire nation into unknown territory, terrified, standing in Moses' shadow:
"Have I not commanded you? [short pause] Be strong and courageous. [short pause] Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go." Joshua 1, verse 9.

Notice the word. [short pause] Commanded. [short pause] Not suggested. [short pause] Not "it would be nice if you could be brave." Commanded. [short pause] Which means courage is not a personality trait you either have or don't. [short pause] It's a decision. [short pause] Something you do, not something you feel.

And then Deuteronomy, same message, different moment: "Be strong and courageous. [short pause] Do not be afraid or terrified because of them, for the Lord your God goes with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you." Deuteronomy 31, verse 6.
Two different books. [short pause] Two different speakers. [short pause] The same command. [short pause] Be courageous. [short pause] Because you are not alone.

So what are you avoiding? [short pause] Name it. [short pause] The conversation you keep rehearsing but never starting. [short pause] The email sitting in drafts. [short pause] The decision that feels too heavy. [short pause] The doctor's appointment. [short pause] The apology. [short pause] The boundary.

Now feel the resistance. [short pause] Where does it live in your body? [short pause] The tightness in your chest? [short pause] The heaviness in your arms? [short pause] The knot in your stomach? [short pause] That's fear... [short pause] [short pause] wearing a costume called "later." Wearing a costume called "I'm not ready." Wearing a costume called "maybe next week."

Here's what the research shows: courage and fear are not opposites. [short pause] They coexist. [short pause] Every act of bravery in human history was performed by someone who was afraid. [short pause] The feeling of fear is not evidence that you shouldn't do something. [short pause] It's often evidence that it matters.
So let's do something with this.

Put your hand on your chest. [short pause] Feel your heartbeat. [short pause] It's steady. [short pause] It's been steady through every hard thing you've ever done. [short pause] Your body already knows how to carry you through fear. [short pause] You just have to let it.

Now close your eyes if they aren't already. [short pause] See yourself doing the thing you named. [short pause] Not the catastrophe version your anxiety plays on repeat. [short pause] The real version.

See yourself walking into it with steady breath. [short pause] Your shoulders are back, not braced. [short pause] Your hands are open, not clenched. [short pause] See yourself speaking clearly, even if your voice shakes a little. [short pause] See yourself in that room. [short pause] That office. [short pause] That hospital. [short pause] That conversation. [short pause] That mirror.

Now see yourself on the other side of it. [short pause] Feel the relief. [short pause] The lightness. [short pause] That quiet dignity of having faced something hard and not run. [short pause] You know that feeling. [short pause] You've had it before. [short pause] After the conversation you dreaded but finally had. [short pause] After the step you almost didn't take. [short pause] Every act of courage you've ever taken started with the exact same resistance you're feeling right now.

There's a moment... [short pause] [short pause] right before courage happens... [short pause] [short pause] where everything in you says "not yet." And courage is simply deciding that "not yet" has had its turn. [short pause] Now is now.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] I'm afraid. [short pause] You know what I'm avoiding, even if I don't want to say it out loud. [short pause] I don't feel brave today. [short pause] But you commanded courage, which means you'll supply what the command requires. [short pause] Go with me into the thing I want to run from. [short pause] Not around it. [short pause] Through it. [short pause] Give me one breath of bravery. [short pause] That's enough to start. [short pause] Amen.
One breath. [short pause] One step. [short pause] One honest word. [short pause] That's all courage asks of you today.

You are commanded to be strong. [short pause] Not because you are alone... [short pause] [short pause] but precisely because you are not. [short pause] The God who goes with you will never leave you, will never forsake you, wherever you go.
Carry that. [short pause] And go with courage.
